preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for a redacted push notification channel derived from CLV-018 roadmap records.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define push channel contracts for redacted outbound notifications, device/topic identity, delivery attempts, disabled states, provider errors, and optional signed action responses.
Select and document an initial provider strategy such as ntfy, Pushover-style provider, or first-party relay, including why it is appropriate for ForgeLink's local-first boundary.
Implement secure credential/device-token storage with rotation/revoke behavior and no token leakage through renderer, logs, diagnostics, exports, or screenshots.
Implement redaction profiles for push payloads so lock-screen-safe content is the default and private message/contact/approval details are not sent unless policy explicitly allows it.
If quick actions ship, require signed action payloads, expiration, replay protection, local pending-action lookup, contact/agent policy checks, and durable outcome records.
Add UI for push setup, test notification, device/topic status, revoke, disabled state, and redaction preview.
Add deterministic tests for delivery success/failure, stale tokens, duplicate action taps, redaction, missing credentials, provider outage, and diagnostics exclusion.
Document operator setup, privacy limitations, lost-device handling, failure modes, and how this relates to the first-party mobile companion.
